title: Corridor 6: Etudes de faisabilité - segment Bangui-Akula
short_description: CORRIDOR 6: ETUDES DE FAISABILITÉ - SEGMENT BANGUI-AKULA
long_description: L¿objectif global du contrat est de contribuer au développement du corridor stratégique prioritaire n°6 « Douala-Kribi-Kampala » (Cameroun, République centrafricaine, République démocratique du Congo, Ouganda) identifié par l¿Union européenne et porteur de multiples opportunités de développement territorial, dans le cadre de l¿initiative européenne Global Gateway. L¿objectif spécifique est la préparation d¿un projet d¿aménagement d¿une partie du Corridor 6 (le segment Bangui-Zongo-Gemena- Akula) dans tous ses aspects (techniques, financiers et organisationnels) afin de permettre sa mise en ¿uvre.  Le contrat doit permettre la réalisation des études de faisabilité nécessaires, ainsi qu¿un programme de renforcement de capacité nécessaire. Enfin, le contrat va préparer le plan de financement du projet identifié.

title: Modernisation du Réseau Routier au Tchad
short_description: MODERNISATION DU RÉSEAU ROUTIER AU TCHAD
channel_reported: European Investment Bank (Trust fund via EIB like FEMIP, NIF, Interest subsidies...)
long_description: The project consists of the design, reconstruction and upgrading of some 229 km of the road corridor from N'Djamena to the Cameroon border in the South. This 596 Km long corridor enables Chad to have access to the nearest seaport located in Douala, Cameroon. The Project also improves the transport conditions, including service level, road safety and climate resilience of a key regional transport corridor enhancing the accessibility of Chad, which is a landlocked country.

title: Enhancing Resilience in Border communities: Climate- adapted Water Resource Management and Feeder Roads (DARIS WACAN)
short_description: ENHANCING RESILIENCE IN BORDER COMMUNITIES: CLIMATE- ADAPTED WATER RESOURCE MANAGEMENT AND FEEDER ROADS (DARIS WACAN)
long_description: The United Nations¿ International Organization for Migration (IOM) submitted a proposal for this contract  the summary is below. Overall Objective of the action: Building climate change resilience and integrated water resource management through improved water infrastructure, rainwater harvesting, better natural resource management, accessibility to market and trade routes.   Specific Objectives of the action: 1.Targeted border communities have increased availability and access to markets and sustainable water for human consumption and their livelihood. 2.Develop livelihood interventions and regional initiatives on water resource management and climate change adaptation to prevent and mitigate impacts on future droughts. Indicative results relating to specific objective 1: Result 1: Communities living along the border areas have increased access to WASH services through construction/ rehabilitation of ground and surface water infrastructures. Result 2: Communities have increased capacity to sustainably manage water resources and related infrastructure through Water Users Associations (WUAs) Result 3 Access and social economic development between border communities is enhanced through construction/rehabilitation of feeder roads in the target locations. Indicative results relating to specific objective 2: Result 4: Border communities are supported through to income-generating livelihoods activities. Result 5. Coordination between government, communities and civil society actors is improved to strengthen cross-border governance.  The purpose of this contract is to implement indicative activities relating to outputs 2.1 and 2.2 under Specific Objective 2 of the EU Alliance for the Horn of Africa Initiative Programme (ACT-62202).

title: EU-LAC Digital Alliance
short_description: EU-LAC DIGITAL ALLIANCE
channel: Economic Commission for Latin America and the Caribbean
channel_reported: Economic Commission for Latin America and the Caribbean
long_description: This action will support the EU-LAC Digital Alliance Regional Team Europe Initiative on Digital transformation and innovation. The Digital Alliance - through the EU-LAC Digital Accelerator (using the D4D hub platform) - will foster bi-regional digital partnerships and create investment opportunities in digital area, boosting at the same time digital innovation, entrepreneurship and competitiveness in LAC. It will do so by fostering a Team Europe approach in synergy with the EU_LAC Digital Accelerator and with the engagement of the private sector and other key stakeholders (e.g. EU MS, industry, civil society organisations, academia, and financial institutions). The digital transformation in LAC requires massive investments in the public sector, but also in the private sector for services and industry. The investment opportunities are planned to be enhanced by mobilizing EFSD+ and blending mechanisms when needed and thanks to the private sectors from both regions. The Alliance will also contribute to the digitalisation of MSMEs that should increase their competitiveness. The consolidation of an EU-LAC network of private sector entities will stimulate the digital transformation and allow smoother sectoral communication and cooperation between EU and LAC businesses. It will enable the exchange of relevant information between business support organisations in order to identify gaps, needs, and opportunities for EU-LAC stakeholders in digital cooperation and innovation. The Accelerator will facilitate an EU-LAC multi-stakeholders dialogue on key topics regarding the digital and data economy that will result in recommendations from stakeholders to governments for improving the enabling environment of the digital economy. The action includes active networking exchanges and events, B2B and G2B events. Within this context, opportunities for partnerships, business ventures and further collaborations will be identified, selected and disseminated. An increased number of collaboration and business partnerships should expand EU-LAC investment flows and joint ventures. At the same time, they will encourage even more policy and regulatory proposals that enable the digital economy and strengthen the institutional collaboration with the eLAC Digital Agenda process.

title: Construction d'un quai maritime de commerce à Leava - île de Futuna
short_description: CONSTRUCTION D'UN QUAI MARITIME DE COMMERCE À LEAVA - ÎLE DE FUTUNA
long_description: Le présent marché de travaux porte sur la construction d'un quai portuaire en eau profonde de 62x18 m surmonté d¿une plate-forme béton à Leava ¿ île de Futuna.   Il est prévu que cet ouvrage principal soit composé d¿un quai sur pieux et de 3 ducs d¿albe d¿amarrage, côté mer, ainsi que d¿un terre-plein en arrière du quai, créé par les remblais des matériaux extraits par dragage, le tout ceinturé par une clôture en rideau mixte et digue d¿enrochement.   Pour faciliter l'exploitation de l'ouvrage principal, la construction d'aménagements annexes est prévue. Il s'agit de :  - la construction d¿une cale de mise à l¿eau de bateaux légers sur remorque,  - la construction d¿une dalle de roulement sur le terre-plein (2200 m²), de caniveaux de récupération, d'unité de traitement de l¿eau et de mats d¿éclairage.

long_description: Ce projet vise la mise en place un observatoire permettant d¿identifier, analyser et publier les pratiques anormales et les irrégularités constatées sur les corridors Douala- N¿Djamena, Douala-Bangui, Yaoundé-Libreville, à une périodicité régulière et à partir de critères de performance définis.    En particulier il s'agira de:    1- Fournir des données fiables, pertinentes et spécifiques sur les obstacles à la fluidité du trafic sur ces corridors      2- Identifier les facteurs qui expliquent les pratiques anormales      3- Susciter les réformes permettant d¿améliorer la fluidité du trafic.

title: Investissements pour le développement de la zone d¿influence du corridor stratégique Abidjan-Ouagadougou ¿ villes secondaires
short_description: INVESTISSEMENTS POUR LE DÉVELOPPEMENT DE LA ZONE D¿INFLUENCE DU CORRIDOR STRATÉGIQUE ABIDJAN-OUAGADOUGOU ¿ VILLES SECONDAIRES
long_description: Cette action vise à renforcer la résilience des villes secondaires par le développement de leurs infrastructures de base.L¿intervention est une contribution pragmatique à une approche territoriale intégrée, ciblant en particulier la durabilité de la croissance des villes secondaires par des infrastructures de base (marchés, voiries urbaines, ...), l¿amélioration de l¿accès à l¿eau potable pour les Personnes Déplacées Internes (PDI) et les Populations hôtes, le renforcement des infrastructures d¿assainissement urbain. Le programme ciblera géographiquement la zone d¿influence du corridor stratégique Abidjan-Ouagadougou, i.e. le « triangle » OKDB (Ouagadougou - Koudougou ¿ Dédougou ¿ Bobo Dioulasso), élargi à la région des Cascades. Concernant les villes secondaires, le programme ciblera Banfora, Dédougou et Koudougou.Cet espace inclut trois villes secondaires dont le développement durable nécessite des investissements en infrastructures. Les territoires concernés, qui hébergent déjà environ 10 % des PDIs du pays et pour lesquels un afflux de plus en plus important est anticipé, restent encore accessibles pour des activités de développement économique et social. La concentration d¿actions multisectorielles sur une zone géographique (approche territoriale), qui a bénéficié au surcroit d¿actions de l¿AAP 2023, doit permettre d¿obtenir plus d¿impact et répondre urgemment à la fragilité des villes secondaires affectées par ces déplacements de population. Le programme sera mis en ¿uvre selon une approche intégrée humanitaire-développement-paix (nexus HDP) travaillant dans une zone qui constitue le grenier du pays et visant à inclure les personnes déplacées internes (PDI) et autres personnes vulnérables, notamment en répondant aux besoins des personnes ayant dû effectuer des déplaceements secondaires sur la zone d¿intervention. Les activités du programme chercheront à assurer la continuité et la durabilité des actions menées en urgence. Les expériences, leçons apprises et analyses de vulnérabilité de la communauté humanitaire contribueront à cibler les activités de l¿action. L¿expérience des États membres dans les zones ciblées sera également prise en compte.L¿action sera mise en ¿uvre principalement par des agences d¿exécution des États membres, dans une approche Team Europe. Des consortia d¿ONGs, en première ligne pour la mise en ¿uvre des actions sur le terrain, seront également impliqués dans l¿exécution de l¿intervention. L¿action représente aussi une contribution au programme de développement des villes secondaires du Burkina Faso par des investissements dans les principales villes secondaires dans la zone d¿influence du corridor stratégique Abidjan-Ouagadougou, en particulier sur le plan de la croissance durable, la création d¿emplois décents et l¿accélération de la transition écologique. L¿action s¿inscrit pleinement dans le TEI « Pacte Vert pour les Jeunes »(notamment par l¿augmentation de l¿employabilité des jeunes). Elle contribue aux engagements de l¿UE envers les personnes déplacées et leurs communautés hôtes

title: NIP - Neighbourhood Investment Platform 2022 - East
short_description: NIP - NEIGHBOURHOOD INVESTMENT PLATFORM 2022 - EAST
long_description: This action is the 2022  annual  contribution  to  the Neighbourhood  Investment  Platform  (NIP) for the Neighbourhood East.The   NIP   forms   an   integral   part   of   the   European   Fund   for   Sustainable   Development  ( EFSD+ )  being  its  blending  arm  in  the  Neighbourhood  in  complementarity  of  budgetary guarantees.The  Overall  Objective  of  the  NIP  is  to  mobilise  investments in  infrastructure  in  transport, energy, environment, with a particular focus on climate change mitigation and adaptation, and to  support  social  and  private  sector  development  in  Eastern  and  Southern  Partner  Countries. In particular, the NIP will support the implementation of the Economic and Investment Plan (EIP) and its country flagships.The  NIP  is  designed  to combine  EU  grants  or  financial  instruments  with  other  public  and private financing. By reducing, through co-financing, the overall cost or risk of the project or by   subsidising   interest   rates   or   financing   technical   assistance   or   providing   financial instruments,  the  NIP  will  encourage  the  partner  country  governments,  private  sector  and/or public institutions to carry out essential investments that would otherwise not happen.

title: EU contribution to Sustainable Transport Connectivity in the Western Balkans 2021-2027 (2021-2027 WBIF)
short_description: EU CONTRIBUTION TO SUSTAINABLE TRANSPORT CONNECTIVITY IN THE WESTERN BALKANS 2021-2027 (2021-2027 WBIF)
long_description: In October 2020 the European Commission adopted a comprehensive Economic and Investment Plan (EIP) for the Western Balkans3, which aims to spur the long-term economic recovery of the region, support a green and digital transition, foster regional integration and convergence with the European Union.The EIP is accompanied by the Guidelines for implementing the Green Agenda for the Western Balkans (GAWB), which further detail investments and actions that can foster the green transition in the region under the EIP and beyond. With the EIP, the Western Balkans Agenda on Innovation, Research, Education, Culture, Youth and Sport and the Green Agenda, the EU provides strong support for economic recovery and reform for a modern, greener and more prosperous Western Balkans delivering better to their citizens on the road to the EU.The EIP also offers a path for a successful regional economic integration to help accelerate convergence with the EU and close the development gap between EU Member States and the Western Balkans region. The EIP will increase the competitiveness of the Western Balkans. Its implementation will have to be accompanied with the relevant reform measures.The EIP Communication identifies ten flagships with the expectation that these would unlock significant investments in particular in the field of digital, sustainable transport, clean energy transition and environment.To support the implementation of mature transport flagship projects, identified in the EIP, this Action foresees the provision of technical assistance and investment grants for the construction or rehabilitation of sustainable infrastructures in the Western Balkans Investment Framework (WBIF) in the period 2021-2027. It also supports the Poznan Summit commitment on the Western Balkans road and rail safety and reduction of travelling time.The Western Balkans Investment Framework members include the European Commission, member states, bilateral donors, financial institutions who altogether in partnership share the objectives of the Economic and Investment Plan for Western Balkans and together contribute to achieve common goals to spur the socio-economic cohesion of the region. In this context, the Western Balkans Investment Framework contributes to support the Team Europe Initiative.

With the EIP, the Western Balkans Agenda on Innovation, Research, Education, Culture, Youth and Sport and the Green Agenda, the EU provides strong support for economic recovery and reform for a modern, greener and more prosperous Western Balkans delivering better to their citizens on the road to the EU.The EIP also offers a path for a successful regional economic integration to help accelerate convergence with the EU and close the development gap between EU Member States and the Western Balkans region. The EIP will increase the competitiveness of the Western Balkans. Its implementation will have to be accompanied with the relevant reform measures.The EIP Communication identifies ten flagships with the expectation that these would unlock significant investments in particular in the field of digital, sustainable transport, clean energy transition and environment.To support the implementation of mature transport flagship projects, identified in the EIP, this Action foresees the provision of technical assistance and investment grants for the construction or rehabilitation of sustainable infrastructures in the Western Balkans Investment Framework (WBIF) in the period 2021-2027. It also supports the Poznan Summit commitment on the Western Balkans road and rail safety and reduction of travelling time.The Western Balkans Investment Framework members include the European Commission, member states, bilateral donors, financial institutions who altogether in partnership share the objectives of the Economic and Investment Plan for Western Balkans and together contribute to achieve common goals to spur the socio-economic cohesion of the region. In this context, the Western Balkans Investment Framework contributes to support the Team Europe Initiative.

title: NIP - Neighbourhood Investment Platform 2021 - East share contribution
short_description: NIP - NEIGHBOURHOOD INVESTMENT PLATFORM 2021 - EAST SHARE CONTRIBUTION
long_description: Neighbourhood Investment Platform (NIP) ¿ 2021 East This action is the 2021 annual contribution to the Neighbourhood Investment Platform (NIP) for the Neighbourhood East. The Overall Objective of the NIP is to facilitate additional investments in infrastructure in transport, energy, environment, with a particular focus on climate change mitigation and adaptation, and to support social and private sector development in Eastern and Southern Partner countries. In particular, the NIP will support the growth of micro, small, and medium size enterprises (MSMEs) by making available a range of financial instruments notably through risk-sharing capital mechanisms.The NIP is designed to combine EU grants or financial instruments with other public and private financing. By reducing, through co-financing, the overall cost or risk of the project or by subsidising interest rates and/or financing technical assistance or providing financial instruments, the NIP will encourage the partner country governments, private sector and/or public institutions to carry out essential investments in sectors which would otherwise be postponed due to lack of resources.The NIP forms an integral part of the European Fund for Sustainable Development ( EFSD ), the purpose of which is to support investments and increase access to financing, primarily in the European Neighbourhood and Africa in order to foster sustainable, inclusive economic and social development and promote the socioeconomic resilience of partner countries.

long_description: EPIC aims at supporting the investment in quality infrastructure projects to enhance transport connectivity within the Eastern Partnership region and with the EU. It will assist governments, municipalities, international and European financial institutions to plan, prepare and implement priority investments on the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T), and related urban nodes and horizontal measures such as traffic management, logistics chains and innovative transport solutions.

title: NIP - Neighbourhood Investment Platform 2024 - East
short_description: NIP - NEIGHBOURHOOD INVESTMENT PLATFORM 2024 - EAST
long_description: The Neighbourhood Investment Platform (NIP) is a blending facility, combining European Union grant contributions or financial instruments, with other public and private sector resources such as loans and equity in order to leverage additional non-grant financing. It forms part of the European Fund for Sustainable Development Plus (EFSD+) as foreseen in Article 32 of the NDICI ¿ Global Europe Regulation. The overall objective of the NIP is to mobilise investments in line with the Economic and Investment Plan (EIP) for the Eastern Partnership and its country flagships. The NIP is designed to combine EU grants or financial instruments with other public and private financing to implement the Economic and Investment Plan. By reducing, through co-financing, the overall cost or risk of the project or by subsidising interest rates or financing technical assistance or providing financial instruments, the NIP will encourage the partner country governments, private sector and/or public institutions to carry out essential investments that would otherwise not happen. The NIP is key to mobilise priority investments in infrastructure in transport, energy, environment, with a particular focus on climate change mitigation and adaptation, and to support gender equality  social and private sector and micro, small, and medium size enterprises (MSMEs) development in Eastern Partner Countries, as identified in the EIP.

long_description: The specific objective of this contract is as follows: To facilitate the smooth and efficient implementation of EU cooperation, engagement, and public diplomacy in Somalia.

title: Latin America and Caribbean Investment Facility LACIF
short_description: LATIN AMERICA AND CARIBBEAN INVESTMENT FACILITY LACIF
long_description: Until 2020, the Latin America (LAIF) and Caribbean (CIF) Facilities have been operating under different budgetary regimes, LAIF under the Development Cooperation Instrument (DCI) and the CIF under the European Development Fund (EDF). This new Facility, LACIF, follows the strategic logic under the new NDICI to merge different financial instruments under the same umbrella. DCI and EDF converge in the new NDICI and this provides the opportunity to combine the two previous facilities in one, taking advantage of shared structures and stakeholders, minimizing administrative transaction costs and adding flexibility to the process of resource allocation, while catering to the unique opportunities and challenges of the two regions. Moreover, action at regional level is particularly relevant to tackle crucial aspects of infrastructure involving networks (transport, energy, water and digital) and regarding cross-border issues (health security, supply chains, migration, etc.). LACIF will continue to boost access to funds from financial institutions, which are supporting key investments for sustainable inclusive infrastructure development with a focus on climate change, as well as rights and gender responsive private sector development in the region contributing substantially to poverty alleviation throughout the continent.European financial institutions were key partners of LAIF and CIF, an important factor for the success of the Facilities was the participation of the regional Latin American and Caribbean financial institutions, more prominently the Inter-American Development Bank (IDB), the Latin American Development Bank (CAF), the Central American Bank for Economic Integration (CABEI) and the Caribbean Development Bank (CDB), acting as co-lead implementers for many operations. This resulted in a stronger and more strategic partnership between European and Latin American financial institutions.

title: EU Support to the aviation sector in Africa
short_description: EU SUPPORT TO THE AVIATION SECTOR IN AFRICA
long_description: The Action consists of technical cooperation in the field of civil aviation, with the objective of developing a safe and sustainable aviation environment on the African continent. The action aims at capacity building for aviation safety, environmental protection and climate action, the introduction of new sustainable technologies and aviation related regulatory infrastructure. Furthermore, it will support the development of the Single African Air Transport Market (SAATM), a process initiated by the African Union and driven by the African Civil Aviation Commission (AFCAC) to develop a single aviation market of intra-African air traffic.The Action will support the following aspects: i) advocate the development of a safe, sustainable and integrated aviation market throughout the continent  ii) improve the regulatory environment  iii) improving capacity to ensure safe and sustainable aviation  and iv) facilitating market access for EU industry by enabling exchange with aviation authorities and African aviation communities.This Action fully contributes to the EU Global Gateway strategy in supporting its pledge to deliver smart and sustainable connectivity, fostering regional and continental integration, improving security and safety of mobility, facilitating trade, while also fostering convergence with European or international technical and environmental standards.

Furthermore, it will support the development of the Single African Air Transport Market (SAATM), a process initiated by the African Union and driven by the African Civil Aviation Commission (AFCAC) to develop a single aviation market of intra-African air traffic.The Action will support the following aspects: i) advocate the development of a safe, sustainable and integrated aviation market throughout the continent  ii) improve the regulatory environment  iii) improving capacity to ensure safe and sustainable aviation  and iv) facilitating market access for EU industry by enabling exchange with aviation authorities and African aviation communities.This Action fully contributes to the EU Global Gateway strategy in supporting its pledge to deliver smart and sustainable connectivity, fostering regional and continental integration, improving security and safety of mobility, facilitating trade, while also fostering convergence with European or international technical and environmental standards.

title: Multiannual Operational Programme on transport in favour of the Republic of North Macedonia for 2024-2027-MK2024-2027MAAP
short_description: MULTIANNUAL OPERATIONAL PROGRAMME ON TRANSPORT IN FAVOUR OF THE REPUBLIC OF NORTH MACEDONIA FOR 2024-2027-MK2024-2027MAAP
long_description: The Multiannual Operational Programme (OP) on transport for the Republic of North Macedonia represents a comprehensive strategy dedicated to improving the country's transport sector while concurrently addressing significant objectives of environment, disaster risk reduction (DRR), climate mitigation and adaptation. This programme seeks to harmonise North Macedonia's transport policies and initiatives with the European Union's (EU) robust standards for a cohesive, sustainable transport network, aligning further with Chapter 14 transport policy and Chapter 21 TEN-T network, as one of the main outcomes. The OP is within the broader ambit of the National Transport Strategy (NTS) 2018-2030. This strategic integration reinforces North Macedonia's commitment to develop a globally coordinated transport sector seamlessly integrated into the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T), significantly bolstering the nation's economic, social, and environmental trajectory.To align North Macedonia's broader development goals with a robust network of EU and international initiatives, the OP demonstrates a solid commitment to various key frameworks and plans. These include the IPA III Programming Framework[1], the 2022 Commission Report on North Macedonia[2], the Economic Reform Programme (ERP) 2023-2025[3], and the Economic Investment Plan (EIP) for the Western Balkans[4]. Additionally, by aligning with the Green Agenda for the Western Balkans[5] and the Five-Year Rolling Work Plan for the Development of the TEN-T in the Western Balkans[6], the OP ensures that North Macedonia's transport sector evolves in line with EU-wide standards. The OP upholds the principles of sustainable development and embraces the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). It envisions fostering innovative and sustainable infrastructure, addressing climate change, enhancing community resilience, and promoting public health and well-being.The OP on transport is centred on three main areas of support:·Rail Transport Infrastructure: This pillar aims to strengthen the capacity, safety, efficiency, and sustainability of the North Macedonia railway transport infrastructure. The focus is primarily on Corridor X, including reconstructing and rehabilitating railway bridges on specific corridor sections and advancing rail safety, policy implementation, and alignment with EU regulation requirements, such as 4th railway package. These endeavours are guided by EU standards, demonstrating North Macedonia¿s commitment to align with EU requirements and contribute to environmental protection, climate resilience, and disaster risk reduction.·<em style= colo

title: Blending 2024
short_description: BLENDING 2024
long_description: The Ukraine Investment Framework is an investment framework aiming at supporting recovery and reconstruction investments in Ukraine, undertaken by Ukrainian authorities, private sector companies, municipalities, state-owned enterprises or other actors. The Ukraine Investment Framework will address priorities identified in the Ukraine Plan and support its objectives and its implementation. It will focus on economic sectors which are identified as strategic in the Ukraine Plan, including access to finance/financial markets, human capital and social sectors, energy, transport, agri-food, critical raw materials, digital transformation, green transition and environmental protection, as well as specific strategic investments and industries. The Ukraine Investment Framework constitutes an integrated financial package supplying financing capacity in the form of financial instruments, budgetary guarantees and blending operations in Ukraine.

title: JASPERS advisory support to Ukraine
short_description: JASPERS ADVISORY SUPPORT TO UKRAINE
long_description: The main objective of the `JASPERS advisory support to Ukraine¿ Action is to provide advisory support to Beneficiaries in Ukraine to help them prepare or implement quality investment projects that are in line with EU and EIB standards. Advisory support offered may span the entire project cycle from sector strategies to project implementation, and includes horizontal support and capacity building activities. Sectors to be covered will include transport, urban regeneration, social housing, environment/ water/ irrigation and solid waste, agrifood, broadband, digital/ telecom and social sectors (health, education).

title: EU-Central Asia Global Gateway:  Support to the development of the Trans-Caspian Transport Corridor
short_description: EU-CENTRAL ASIA GLOBAL GATEWAY:  SUPPORT TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE TRANS-CASPIAN TRANSPORT CORRIDOR
long_description: The Action focuses on improving soft connectivity and gender-responsive regulatory frameworks in Central Asia and along the Trans Caspian Transport Corridor (TCTC), improving intra-regional cooperation and coordination in Central Asia and along the TCTC and facilitating investments in green and climate-proofed key infrastructure of the TCTC. To achieve this it will provide technical assistance to Central Asian governments and possibly other relevant stakeholders (e.g. private transport and logistic operators) at both regional and national levels. The Action will advance regulatory and legislative reforms and assist with improving soft connectivity aspects in the TCTC, such as customs procedures, harmonisation of documentation, safety and security standards, interoperability aspects, market and sector transformation and logistics coordination, taking into consideration greening opportunities and climate change mitigation and adaptation measures. In order to facilitate an inclusive and coordinated approach of investments and reforms along the TCTC, the programme will also provide support services to a TCTC Coordination Platform that will issue strategic guidance for the Action implementation. This Platform was launched on 12 June 2024, as a direct follow-up to the EU-Central Asia Transport Investors Forum held in January 2024. Finally, to unlock investment and accelerate the development of the TCTC, the programme will support preparatory studies for potential projects, assessing options for sustainable and low-carbon resilient pathways for the green transition. By removing soft connectivity obstacles and supporting the coordination and acceleration of climate-proofed infrastructure investments, the programme will help increase regional trade and sustainably reduce land transport times, aiming for a less carbon-intensive connection between Asia and Europe.

title: Sustainable mobility: urban nodes
short_description: SUSTAINABLE MOBILITY: URBAN NODES
long_description: The Overall Objective of this Action is to support the global transition to inclusive, clean and climate-neutral sustainable urban mobility systems that are also efficient and safe. The Specific(s) Objective(s) of this action are to:  1) accelerate investments in low-carbon urban mobility solutions, in the context of Global Gateway strategy. 2) improve knowledge and dissemination of best practices, particularly European best practices, on sustainable urban mobility in beneficiary countries, including regarding most vulnerable people and women mobility  3) improve policy dialogue and partnerships on sustainable low-carbon urban mobility in beneficiary countries, particularly within the framework of the European «Green Agenda».

The project purpose is to develop and implement harmonised road transport policies, laws, regulations and standards for efficient cross border road transport and transit networks, transport and logistics services, systems and procedures in the EA-SA region. short_description: FINAL EVALUATION OF NAIROBI MISSING LINK ROADS AND NON-MOTORISED TRANSPORT FACILITIES
long_description: This Request for Service contains the final evaluation of the Action: KENYA/ACP/Regional Economic Integration by means of Transport Infrastructure - Urban Roads (Nairobi Missing Link Roads and Non-Motorised transport facilities). The main objectives of this evaluation are to provide an overall independent assessment of the past performance of the Action, paying particular attention to its results measured against its expected objectives and the reasons underpinning such results  and key lessons learned, conclusions and related recommendations in order to improve current and future Interventions. This evaluation will serve the EU Delegation and the Government of Kenya to make an informed judgment about the contribution of the Nairobi Missing Links Road project, to the objectives set in the Nairobi metropolitan transport improvement programme.The evaluation will assess the intervention using the six standard DAC evaluation criteria.

title: Technical Assistance to the Ukraine Urban Road Safety
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO THE UKRAINE URBAN ROAD SAFETY
channel_reported: European Investment Bank (Trust fund via EIB like FEMIP, NIF, Interest subsidies...)
long_description: The Project is expected to result in the improvement of road safety and reduced risks, in particular for vulnerable road users. The schemes implemented will also encourage walking, cycling, and use of public transport in each city. The knowledge about safety engineering and traffic calming options in each participating city will be increased, as road designers are given training on modern international design practices and they gain experience in it. The Ukraine Urban Road Safety Project will contribute to urban road safety investment programmes in the participating cities, namely Odessa, Dnipro, Lviv, Kiev, and Kharkiv. The Project will complement and build upon other ongoing road safety initiatives at national level including those supported by the EU and the Bank, and provide safer road infrastructure with a focus on better facilities for pedestrians and cyclists, and better access to public transport and thus promote sustainable urban transport solutions.

short_description: ETUDES, SURVEILLANCE ET CONTRÔLE DES TRAVAUX DE BÂTIMENTS, D`AMÉNAGEMENTS NÉCESSAIRES ET D`ÉQUIPEMENT DU CENTRE NATIONAL DE L'ENVIRONNEMENT ET DE LA S
long_description: L'objectif général est de contribuer au développement économique et social par l'amélioration du transport routier à travers la protection de l'environnement et l'amélioration de la sécurité routière par la relance du contrôle technique automobile en République de Guinée.    Les objectifs particuliers sont les suivants :    -Établir l'état des lieux du site devant accueillir le Centre National Environnement et Sécurité Automobile (CNESA)      -Élaborer les études des bâtiments, des ouvrages de Génie Civil, des équipements et installations nécessaires afin de permettre le démarrage des travaux de construction et d'installations des techniques pour le contrôle technique des véhicules      -Réunir les préalables nécessaire à la réalisation des infrastructures (mise à disposition des terrains, titres de propriété, permis de construire)      -Faciliter le processus d'attribution des offres de marché de travaux et installations des équipements techniques      -Réceptionner et mettre à disposition de l'entreprise les sites destinés à accueillir l'infrastructure      -Assurer le contrôle général des travaux et l'installation des équipements techniques : implanatation, méthodes de mise en oeuvre, matériel utilisé et études sectorielles nécessaires, approbation des plans d'exécution      -Assurer la réception technique des travaux et établir les attachements et les décomptes des travaux      -Procéder à la réception technique des matériaux et des équipements techniques entrant dans la réalisation de l'infrastructure      -Adapter les études aux conditions réelles de terrain et maîtriser les montants contractuels des travaux et des équipements techniques      -Faire les études de sol et levés topographiques éventuels nécessaires      -Assurer le contrôle et la surveillance des travaux jusqu'à la réception définitive.

title: Projet de désenclavement urbain d¿axes de la voirie urbaine à Conakry (SoCaSa 7 à 8).
short_description: PROJET DE DÉSENCLAVEMENT URBAIN D¿AXES DE LA VOIRIE URBAINE À CONAKRY (SOCASA 7 À 8).
channel_reported: United Nations Office for Project Services
long_description: L¿objectif général du projet est de contribuer au développement économique et social du pays post-COVID-19, à la lutte contre la pauvreté, et à l¿intégration régionale à travers un réseau routier de qualité, géré et entretenu de façon appropriée et durable.     Objectifs spécifiques     Les objectifs spécifiques du projet sont :   &#9679 OS 1 : Désenclaver un ou plusieurs quartiers de Conakry à travers la réhabilitation durable d¿un axe Conakry, notamment pour favoriser l¿accessibilité des femmes et des personnes de mobilité réduite      &#9679 OS 2 : Favoriser la relance de l¿économie locale par la création de revenus pour les populations à travers l¿utilisation de la méthode HIMO    &#9679 OS 3 : Renforcer durablement la gestion et l¿entretien de l¿axe routier par l¿engagement des communautés locales.        Résultats attendus     Les résultats attendus du projet sont :   &#9679 Résultat 1 ¿ Construction d'un axe routier long de 2.91km : le réseau routier est renforcé à travers le réaménagement d¿un axe de la voirie urbaine secondaire de Conakry      &#9679 Résultat 2 ¿ Création de revenus à travers la méthode HIMO : les communautés locales bénéficient économiquement et socialement du projet à travers la création d¿emploi HIMO    &#9679 Résultat 3 ¿ Création et renforcement des capacités des comités de gestion : des comités de gestion pour la maintenance de l¿axe sont créés et leurs capacités renforcées.

title: Travaux de construction et équipements d¿un Centre national d¿environnement et de sécurité automobile (CNESA).
short_description: TRAVAUX DE CONSTRUCTION ET ÉQUIPEMENTS D¿UN CENTRE NATIONAL D¿ENVIRONNEMENT ET DE SÉCURITÉ AUTOMOBILE (CNESA).
geography: République de Guinée, Préfecture de Coyah, Sous-préfecture de Kouriah.
long_description: Le contrat consiste en des travaux de construction de bâtiments, d¿aménagements et d¿équipements du Centre national d¿environnement et de sécurité automobile (CNESA).  Le CNESA est conçu pour la relance du contrôle technique automobile, la formation du personnel des Centres d¿environnement et de sécurité automobile (CESA), la formation des conducteurs routiers professionnels et constitue un appui conseil auprès du ministère des Transports pour l¿instruction des dossiers d¿agrément des opérateurs privés.  Il disposera d¿un hangar de contrôle technique de deux lignes de contrôle dont une ligne pour les poids lourds (PL) et l¿autre ligne pour les véhicules légers (VL)  d¿une direction technique, d¿une direction générale, d¿un bâtiment pour un logement, d¿un restaurant, des blocs latrines, d¿un local groupe électrogène, de fosses septiques avec puits perdu, d¿un forage avec château d¿eau, de guérites, d¿un local de formation à la gestion des Centres d¿environnement et de sécurité automobile (CESA) et d¿une clôture.  Une route d¿accès au centre à partir de la route nationale n°1 sera aménagée ainsi que la voirie et des réseaux divers de viabilisation du centre.  Le CNESA sera aussi équipé en matériels de contrôle technique automobile.

short_description: ANAMA/MALI FRONTIER ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The project aims at developing the transport services on the main road network of the country, and insuring traffic safety. The project also aims at promoting trade between different regions of the country, and between Mauritania and its neighboring countries, through the completion of the connecting road between the city of Néma and the borders of Mali. The project is expected to contribute to the development of areas adjacent to the road, integrate isolated localities and improve their access to basic services.

title: NOUAKCHOTT NEW INT. AIRPORT
short_description: NOUAKCHOTT NEW INT. AIRPORT
long_description: The project aims at developing air transport in Mauritania and accommodating national and international flights through the construction of a new international airport in Nouakchott able to handle the increasing demand for air transport. This project will help stimulate the economic and social development of the country.

title: Rehabilitation of the Potlemite - Alaq road
short_description: REHABILITATION OF THE POTLEMITE - ALAQ ROAD
long_description: The project, which is expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2022, consists of the rehabilitation and strengthening of the Amal road section, which extends from Boutlimit to Aleg, with a total length of about 112 km, and a width of 10 m, including 7 m asphalted pavement.

title: Tidjikja – Kiffa – Selibaby – Mali Border Road
short_description: TIDJIKJA – KIFFA – SELIBABY – MALI BORDER ROAD
long_description: The project aims at contributing to the development of land transport services on the main road network in the country, ensuring traffic safety, and stimulating trade between various regions of the country, and between Mauritania and neighboring countries, by providing an internal axis to improve the connection between the north, center and south of the country.

long_description: REGIONAL AND RURAL ROADS NETWORK

title: REGIONAL AND RURAL ROADS NETWORK - PHASE II
short_description: REGIONAL AND RURAL ROADS NETWORK - PHASE II
channel_reported: Ministry of Equipment, Housing and Spatial Planning
long_description: The project aims at linking the rural areas to towns and cities through the construction and rehabilitation of a number of regional and rural roads, in order to increase the safety on the roads, and facilitate land transportation between the agricultural and industrial areas.

title: OUED-ZARGA- BOU SALEM HIGHWAY
short_description: OUED-ZARGA- BOU SALEM HIGHWAY
long_description: The project aims at improving land transportation services on the road network in Tunisia to accommodate the rapidly growing road traffic and reduce traffic accidents. The project also contributes to the economic and social development of the north western region of Tunisia. The Project will develop road linkage with the neighboring Arab Maghreb countries, as it is a part of the Arab Maghreb Motorway

title: REGIONAL and RURAL ROADS
short_description: REGIONAL AND RURAL ROADS
channel_reported: Ministry of Equipment, Housing and Spatial Planning
long_description: The project aims at improving land transportation in a number of Tunisian governorates, and linking agricultural production areas with markets and export facilities. This will be achieved through the construction and rehabilitation of a number of regional and rural roads. The Arab Fund has also financed the first two phases of the project

title: REHABILITATION OF ROADS NETWORK&RURAL ROADS
short_description: REHABILITATION OF ROADS NETWORK&RURAL ROADS
channel_reported: Ministry of Equipment, Housing and Spatial Planning
long_description: The project aims at improving land transportation services and traffic safety on roads in most of the Tunisian governorates, linking agricultural production areas with markets and export facilities, and alleviating the isolation of rural areas. This will be achieved through the increase in the efficiency of the classified road network, and the construction and improvement of a large number of rural roads

title: CONSTRUCTION OF BRIDGES ON CLASSIFIED ROADS ROADS
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF BRIDGES ON CLASSIFIED ROADS ROADS
channel_reported: Ministry of Equipment, Housing and Spatial Planning
long_description: The project aims at improving land transportation services and traffic safety on roads in the Republic of Tunisia. This will be achieved through the construction of a number of bridges on classified roads in most governorates. The project will lead to enhancement of the classified road network efficiency, alleviation of traffic bottlenecks, and improvement of traffic circulation.

title: TUNIS-JELMA HIGHWAY
short_description: TUNIS-JELMA HIGHWAY
long_description: The project, which is expected to be completed by end of 2021, consists of the construction of the Tunis – Jelma motorway, which has a length of about 186 km and consists of 4 lanes, 2 in each direction, with a width of 7 m, a median dividing the two directions with a width ranging between 7 and 12 m, and outer shoulders on both sides of the road with a width of 3 m on each side. The project also consists of the rehabilitation of a connection of about 16 km to link the motorway with the city of El Fahs in the governorate of Zaghouan.

title: IP- Governance Portfolio_Integrated Support for Advancing Reforms and Democratic Governance in Bangladesh (SARG)
short_description: IP- GOVERNANCE PORTFOLIO_INTEGRATED SUPPORT FOR ADVANCING REFORMS AND DEMOCRATIC GOVERNANCE IN BANGLADESH (SARG)
long_description: Following the establishment of an Interim Government in August 2024, this 18-month Initiation Plan (IP) aims to enhance the capabilities of interim authorities, national institutions, and civil society in Bangladesh to drive democratic reforms and foster inclusive development. It houses the early phase of UNDP support for democratic and institutional reforms in Bangladesh and the advancement and repositioning of ongoing governance programming. Central to this initiative is a commitment to restoring trust among stakeholders, engaging systematically with young people, civil society and citizens, and ensuring risk-informed and collaborative approaches. This IP will also serve as a foundational framework to consolidate and align UNDP's democratic governance programming in Bangladesh, and for transitioning to a longer-term portfolio of strategic interventions thereafter.

title: Procurement of medicines necessary for prevention and treatment of non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and protection of maternal and child health in Turkmenistan in 2023-2024
short_description: PROCUREMENT OF MEDICINES NECESSARY FOR PREVENTION AND TREATMENT OF NON-COMMUNICABLE DISEASES (NCDS) AND PROTECTION OF MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH IN TU
long_description: The aim of this project is to strengthen the national health systems capacities to control NCDs, rare diseases, and improve maternal and newborn health in Turkmenistan by ensuring access to free, good quality, available, affordable medicines across the country. The project will provide access to good quality, available, affordable medicines through procurement of medicines for treatment of NCD and other diseases.

title: Construction of Sanam-Tebaram Road Project - reinstated after cancellation
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF SANAM-TEBARAM ROAD PROJECT - REINSTATED AFTER CANCELLATION
long_description: The project aims at improving the physical access of rural population to markets and services, at the same time improving national and regional integration by means of an all_seasoned paved road. The project on completion will result in average journey time from Niamey to Tahoua from 8 hours to 3.5 hours. In addition, vehicle operating cost if expected to reduce from FCFA 1,107 per vehicle_km in 2014 to FCFA 484 per vehicle_km in 2020.

title: Construction of two Regional Integration Roads Dabola-Kouroussa and Guekedou-Kissoudougou-Kondembradou
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF TWO REGIONAL INTEGRATION ROADS DABOLA-KOUROUSSA AND GUEKEDOU-KISSOUDOUGOU-KONDEMBRADOU
long_description: The objective of the project is to establish an efficient connection between Conakry and the neighbouring countries, but also to facilitate trade exchanges with Mali, Côte d'Ivoire, Sierra Leone and Liberia. The project will contribute to stimulate economic growth of the country and to the objectives of the government's Poverty Reduction Strategy Program. The project comprises two road sections, which are part of priority roads program of the Government: i) RN1 Dabola-Kouroussa section (151km) in Upper Guinea, ii) RN2 Kissidougou-Guékédou –Kondembadou (53 km) in Guinéea Forest region.

title: Construction of Labe-Mali Road Project
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF LABE-MALI ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The objective of the project is to improve the human endowment gaps through facilitating physical access of rural population, particularly women, youth and vulnerable groups to markets, health, education, and social protection services, and Facilitate the development of trade between Guinea and Senegal by opening and developing vast agricultural areas and huge unexploited potential.

title: Batchenga-Ntui-Yoko-Tibati-N'gaoundere Corridor Phase II: Construction of Ngatt-Febadi-Likok Road Section Project
short_description: BATCHENGA-NTUI-YOKO-TIBATI-N'GAOUNDERE CORRIDOR PHASE II: CONSTRUCTION OF NGATT-FEBADI-LIKOK ROAD SECTION PROJECT
long_description: The Project Development Objective (PDO) is to help open-up and develop the Centre and Adamaoua Regions of Cameroon by improving the level of road service between Batchenga and Ngaoundere, of which the Ngatt – Fibadi – Likok road is a critical link and thereby enhance the living conditions of the people of these regions. This objective is directly relevant to the problem statement, i.e. the poor condition and low level of service of the existing roads, which hinders the socio-economic development of the region and exposes road users to high risks of accidents and insecurity. The objective is also in line with the country's development objectives, which aim to promote regional integration, economic diversification and social inclusion through improved transports infrastructures and services to facilitate trade exchanges between Cameroon, Nigeria, Chad and the Central African Republic.

title: Construction of the Guinea-Senegal Regional Road Corridor (Section Mali- Gadalougué) Republic of Guinea
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF THE GUINEA-SENEGAL REGIONAL ROAD CORRIDOR (SECTION MALI- GADALOUGUÉ) REPUBLIC OF GUINEA
long_description: The development objective of the project is to contribute to increasing regional trade by enhancing the service level of regional roads and supporting population resilience. It aims to facilitate the rapid sale of agro-pastoral products, strengthen cultural exchanges, and promote social cohesion at national and sub-regional levels. At the sectoral level, the project will enhance the performance of the national and regional transport logistics chain, as well as the development of related services and trades, by improving the level and quality of road service along the corridor linking the ports of Dakar and Conakry. Specifically, the project aims to: (i) Improve the service level of the Mali-Senegal/Guinea-Kédougou border road, thereby reducing the cost of transporting goods, vehicle operating costs, and travel time. (ii) Enhance cargo tracking systems. (iii) Ensure the availability of transport services and facilitate connectivity in agro-sylvo-pastoral areas (iv) Improve living conditions for populations within the Project's Zone of Direct Influence (PZDI).

title: Upgrading of Beterou-Tchaourou-Nigeria Border Road Project
short_description: UPGRADING OF BETEROU-TCHAOUROU-NIGERIA BORDER ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The main objectives of the project are to provide accessibility to the Borgou Province and boost commercial exchange between Benin, Togo, Burkina Faso and Nigeria. It will improve accessibility to markets in the project zone and ensure easier movement of people and goods under improved safety conditions

title: IsDB collaboration with ESID and ESCWA on Safe and Inclusive Road Design in North and West Africa Project
short_description: ISDB COLLABORATION WITH ESID AND ESCWA ON SAFE AND INCLUSIVE ROAD DESIGN IN NORTH AND WEST AFRICA PROJECT
channel: Banks (in recipient country) (deposit taking corporations except Micro Finance Institutions)
channel_reported: Banks (deposit taking corporations except Micro Finance Institutions)
long_description: The project will support 3 IsDB MCs (Morocco, Mauritania and Senegal) to review their geometric design standards/manuals/guidelines in order to ensure that roads are designed to provide adequate levels of safety and accessibility to all road users. In addition to IsDB, the key partners of the project include (a) United Nations Economic Commission for Africa, (b) International Road Federation (IRF), (c) National Road Safety Agency Morocco (NARSA), (d) National Road Safety Agency Senegal (ANASER), (e) Ministry of Works & Transport Mauritania, (f) Ministry of Transport/Works of Morocco, (g) Ministry of Infrastructure & Transport, Senegal, (h) International Road Assessment Program (iRAP) and (i) European Investment Bank (EIB). A close coordination is also included with Tunisia to build on the work carried out by the EU-funded Safer Africa project. The project will be implemented over a 3-year period.

title: Gan-Hithadhoo Link Road of the First Regional Development Project
short_description: GAN-HITHADHOO LINK ROAD OF THE FIRST REGIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROJECT
long_description: The project aims at developing the Southern Region centered on the Gan to Hithadhoo island chain in Seenu Atoll to reduce the in_migration to Male which is at constant pressure on its resources and associated demand for water, power, sewerage, solid waste and social services seriously threaten the environment and qualityof life. The Gan_Hithadhoo Link Road is a part of the infrastructure requirements to develop the region. The project comprises of: connstruction of 17 Km Road from Gan to Hithadhoo, social infrastructure, environmental improvement and institutional building for the project management. - Reflow

title: Reconstruction of Kambia – Tomparie – Kamakwie Road Under TDCP, Sierra Leone
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION OF KAMBIA – TOMPARIE – KAMAKWIE ROAD UNDER TDCP, SIERRA LEONE
long_description: The project aims to enhance Sierra Leone's socio-economic development through the deployment of soil stabilization technology in order to improve access to social, economic infrastructure and regional integration. Key outcomes include significant reductions in travel times, vehicle operating costs, improved road safety, reduced implementation cost (Improved Value for Money), and improved rural connectivity. It also focuses on improving access to safe social amenities like schools and health centers while enhancing national capacity to deploy soil stabilization technology. The tangible outputs include but are not limited to the reconstruction 105 km of road with related structures, ICT equipment, trainings, road safety and capacity building for women in trade facilitation.

long_description: The objective of the project is to stimulate the economic growth of the country by increasing mode options through providing sustainable transport solutions and improving efficiency in the transport sector and thereby promote urban development outside the congested areas of Dakar city centre.

long_description: The project aims at improving transport efficiency and promoting economic development in Uzbekistan through the reconstruction and upgrading the M39 road Almaty-Bishkek-Tashkent¬ Termez in Surkhandarya Region. - Reflow

title: Reconstruction and Upgrading of M39 Road, 1255-1315 km
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION AND UPGRADING OF M39 ROAD, 1255-1315 KM
long_description: The project development objective is to improve the efficiency of the road transport infrastructure and ensure the safe movement of goods and people along the project road section. To reach this objective, the project will reconstruct and upgrade 63 km of road, install Weigh in Motion (WIM) systems for dynamic weight and dimension control as well as carry out various capacity development activities to enhance road transport planning. - Reflow

title: Construction of Guiba-Garango Road Project
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF GUIBA-GARANGO ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The Project development objective is to improve connectivity between the major transport corridors, which at the same time facilitates local commercial activities within the project area to sustain economic growth. The specific objectives are established as follows: i) reduce journey time, and ii) reduce vehicle operating costs. - Reflow

title: Abia State Integrated Infrastructure Development Project
short_description: ABIA STATE INTEGRATED INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT PROJECT
long_description: The development objectives of the project are to contribute to socio-economic development, and improved standard of living through an improved and sustainable transport system linking centers of economic activity and enhanced access to social services.

title: Reconstruction of A-373 Road Project (0-37km)
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION OF A-373 ROAD PROJECT (0-37KM)
long_description: The project development objective is to upgrade and improve condition of A373 road section to accommodate current and future traffic, enhance regional and local connectivity and improve safety on road. The project will also contribute to development of regional trade/tourism and integration with neighboring countries. This will be achieved by upgrading and reconstructing the existing section of A373 road (37 km).

title: Construction of Qukes-Qafe Plloce (Choocus-Chalf Ploch) Section of Tirana-Korca Road Corridor Project - Additional Financing
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF QUKES-QAFE PLLOCE (CHOOCUS-CHALF PLOCH) SECTION OF TIRANA-KORCA ROAD CORRIDOR PROJECT - ADDITIONAL FINANCING
long_description: The project aims to provide the shortest earthquake resilient all-weather road link with the southern isolated villages, borders of North Macedonia and Greece and the rest of Europe within the inner Balkans by reducing travel time, vehicle operating cost and increase comfort, safety and reliability, meet the current and future transport demands, and  help accelerate socioeconomic activities by improving access of people to social amenities, businesses and employment opportunities, trade, tourism resulting from enhanced internal and regional interconnectivity.

title: Reconstruction and Upgrading of M39 Road, 1255-1315 km
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION AND UPGRADING OF M39 ROAD, 1255-1315 KM
long_description: The project development objective is to improve the efficiency of the road transport infrastructure and ensure the safe movement of goods and people along the project road section. To reach this objective, the project will reconstruct and upgrade 63 km of road, install Weigh in Motion (WIM) systems for dynamic weight and dimension control as well as carry out various capacity development activities to enhance road transport planning.

short_description: RECONSTRUCTION OF KAMBIA – TOMPARIE – KAMAKWIE ROAD UNDER TDCP, SIERRA LEONE
long_description: The project aims to enhance Sierra Leone's socio-economic development through the deployment of soil stabilization technology in order to improve access to social, economic infrastructure and regional integration. Key outcomes include significant reductions in travel times, vehicle operating costs, improved road safety, reduced implementation cost (Improved Value for Money), and improved rural connectivity. It also focuses on improving access to safe social amenities like schools and health centers while enhancing national capacity to deploy soil stabilization technology. The tangible outputs include but are not limited to the reconstruction 105 km of road with related structures, ICT equipment, trainings, road safety and capacity building for women in trade facilitation.

title: BAD0090 - Supporting road and infrastructure projects in the Gaza Strip
short_description: BAD0090 - SUPPORTING ROAD AND INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS IN THE GAZA STRIP
channel: Banks (in recipient country) (deposit taking corporations except Micro Finance Institutions)
channel_reported: Banks (deposit taking corporations except Micro Finance Institutions)
long_description: 1- Facilitating the movement of citizens and employees to reach their places of work and residence, alleviating their burdens and facilitatingTraffic and pedestrian traffic.2 - Contribute to saving time to travel from different streets to main streets without obstacles or useAlternate bypass methods.3 - Reviving the private sector and providing job opportunities for the unemployed in the implementation of various projects.4 - Preserving public health by not reducing dust significantly, and getting rid of rainwater accumulations in the areawinter season.5- Reducing congestion, traffic accidents, dings and damage to vehicles used on the roads, and hills are damaged byRoughness amid existing streets, bumps and potholes.6- Reducing environmental and toxic pollution and the spread of health hazards, diseases, rodents, flies and harmful insects.The result of solid waste stacking.7-Obtaining a modern, environmentally safe station for solid waste collection and transfer station in Gaza City.

title: Queen Aalia Airport Expansion Project
short_description: QUEEN AALIA AIRPORT EXPANSION PROJECT
long_description: IDB had approved US$100 million financing for the project in 2007. This commitment was later reduced by US$15 million, which was syndicated to OFID.  The project aimed at upgrading the airside infrastructure and managing the existing operations at the then terminal along with the construction of a new modern terminal at the same location.  The current financing is a supplementary financing to cater the expected grwoth in actual traffic over the coming years from 7 million passengers to 12 million passengers. - Reflow

title: Reconstruction and Upgrading of M39 Road, 1255-1315 km
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION AND UPGRADING OF M39 ROAD, 1255-1315 KM
long_description: The project development objective is to improve the efficiency of the road transport infrastructure and ensure the safe movement of goods and people along the project road section. To reach this objective, the project will reconstruct and upgrade 63 km of road, install Weigh in Motion (WIM) systems for dynamic weight and dimension control as well as carry out various capacity development activities to enhance road transport planning.

title: Nakkas – Basaksehir Motorway Project
short_description: NAKKAS – BASAKSEHIR MOTORWAY PROJECT
long_description: The Project is expected to enhance economic activity in districts of Istanbul it is covering and contribute to the overall social and economic development. The Project Development Objective is to achieve benefits in terms of connectivity enhancement, alleviation of traffic congestion, emissions reductions, enhanced road safety conditions, reduction in travel time costs and new job creation in wider Istanbul area.

title: Inter-Island Connectivity Project
short_description: INTER-ISLAND CONNECTIVITY PROJECT
long_description: The Project is focused on improving maritime transport connectivity, resilience and safety between the islands, supporting economic integration in Comoros. Comoros has various untapped growth potentials, but such potential areas remain fragmented because of poor transport connectivity among and within the islands. Poverty is persistently high particularly in remote areas, with regional inequality widening. In addition, Comoros is highly vulnerable to climatic events and natural hazards, such as tropical cyclones, high waves and coastal flooding. Maritime transport safety is becoming a particular concern for local users. The Project addresses efficiency, reliability and safety in both formal and informal (called kwassa kwassa) maritime transport sectors.The main project objectives are: - Increased economic resilience by facilitating economic internal exchanges between island as well as external exchanges.- Reduce disparities in transport infrastructure and improve social well-being.- Induce increased demand.- Reduce the cost of transportation for people and goods,- Reduce travel time, - Ensure the safety of maritime transport users.

title: Construction of Kyzylorda Bypass Road
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF KYZYLORDA BYPASS ROAD
long_description: The project development objective is to improve the quality of life of the people living along the project road as well as contribute to the development of the international transit traffic potential of the country. The project will improve the efficiency of the road transport infrastructure and ensure the safe and efficient movement of goods and people along the project. The project will eliminate the current road efficiency constraints, which is caused by the poor design and condition of the road. Eventually, average travel time as well as vehicle operating costs will decrease by one third. Additionally, urban traffic will be eased from transit traffic in Kyzylorda city, which will help reduce in city flow of traffic.

title: Eastern Turkiye Middle Corridor Railway Development Project
short_description: EASTERN TURKIYE MIDDLE CORRIDOR RAILWAY DEVELOPMENT PROJECT
long_description: The project aims to contribute to the safe, accessible and sustainable transport systems in Türkiye. The Project Development Objective (PDO) is to improve logistics efficiency along the middle corridor between Divri?i and the Georgia border and to enhance the resilience, connectivity and sustainability of Türkiye's national railway network.

title: Nakkas – Basaksehir Motorway Project
short_description: NAKKAS – BASAKSEHIR MOTORWAY PROJECT
long_description: The Project is expected to enhance economic activity in districts of Istanbul it is covering and contribute to the overall social and economic development. The Project Development Objective is to achieve benefits in terms of connectivity enhancement, alleviation of traffic congestion, emissions reductions, enhanced road safety conditions, reduction in travel time costs and new job creation in wider Istanbul area.

long_description: The general objective of the overall project (phase I and Phase II) is to contribute to the economic growth of the country by supporting the transport development objective of Increasing the percentage of the primary road network in good condition from 80% to 100% fixed by the country National Development Plant 2018-2021 (NDP). long_description: The objective of the project is to facilitate the socio­ economic development of Uganda by upgrading the road link between Rwenkunye- Apac- Lira - Acholibur by improving road surface, road safety and increasing the overall efficiency of road transport system.The development of this road will enhance Uganda's competitiveness as a regional hub linking the neighboring countries. title: Reconstruction of Guliston-Farkhor-Panj-Dusti Road - Phase I
short_description: RECONSTRUCTION OF GULISTON-FARKHOR-PANJ-DUSTI ROAD - PHASE I
long_description: The project development objective is to improve the quality of life of the people living along the project road as well as contribute to the development of the international transit traffic potential of the country. The project will eliminate the current road efficiency constraints, which are caused by the poor design and condition of the road. Eventually, average travel time as well as vehicle operating costs will decrease by one third. To reach the above impact and objective, the project will reconstruct and upgrade 29.6 km of road, pave additional 10 km of connecting roads and install Weigh in Motion (WIM) systems for dynamic weight and dimension control.Key development results expected from the project include:(i)(Outcome level – expected to be achieved by 2029) (i) People with improved access to transportation services increased by 80,000 (Core Sector Indicator – CSI), (ii) Travel time on the rehabilitated road section reduced by 33% to 24 min, (iii) Severe or fatal accidents reduced by 40% to 3 on the road section, (iv) Annual Average Daily Traffic accommodated increased, (v) Average speed on the road increased by 60% towards 80 km/h, (vi) Riding quality (Road roughness -IRI) improved to 2.0 and (vii) 100 new jobs created through improved economic activity (CSI).(ii)(Output level – expected to be achie - Reflow

long_description: THIS PROJECT AIMS TO PROVIDE CONTINUOUS, RELIABLE AND DIRECT LAND TRANSPORT SERVICE BETWEEN MURGAB AND KARAKORUM HIGHWAY THROUGH KULMA PASS. title: Widening of Bertil – Harding Highway Phase II Project
short_description: WIDENING OF BERTIL – HARDING HIGHWAY PHASE II PROJECT
long_description: The general objective of the overall project (phase I and Phase II) is to contribute to the economic growth of the country by supporting the transport development objective of Increasing the percentage of the primary road network in good condition from 80% to 100% fixed by the country National Development Plant 2018-2021 (NDP). Moreover, the NDP, under the objective the enhanced Land, River, Sea and Air Transport for Affordability, Accessibility, and Competitiveness, Government planned to: review and restructure transport sector policies and institutional framework, develop and implement an integrated transport sector in line with the land use master plan, expand, improve, and maintain the road networks, develop Urban Transport, implement the Banjul port expansion project and develop the inland water ways, implement the Airport Improvement Project, and implement airport hub and city planThe specific objectives of the proposed project phase II are (i) to ensure dealing with the increasing traffic growth of more than 3% annually on the Bertil – Harding highway which is expected to reach 28,000 vehicle/day by 2040 (16,000 vehicle/day in 2021), and (ii) to enhance the resilience of the highway infrastructure (under construction in phase I) by (a) Improving the quality of the asphalt, (b) maximizing the connectiv

title: Center-West Region Roads project
short_description: CENTER-WEST REGION ROADS PROJECT
long_description: The objective of the project is to stimulate economic growth of the country and contribute indirectly to the objectives of the government's Poverty Reduction Strategy Program. The project would contribute to the economic and social development of the center-west region and increase the competitiveness of the Ivoirian economy in the West African sub-region. On the international level, the project would contribute to promoting regional integration between Cote d'Ivoire, Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone.

title: Upgrading of Muyembe Nakapiripirit Road Project
short_description: UPGRADING OF MUYEMBE NAKAPIRIPIRIT ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The overarching development objective is to facilitate socio-economic development by upgrading the earthen/gravel road links to (a) improve safety, (b) meet the present and future transportation demands and (c) enhance the overall efficiency of road transportation system.The objective of the project is to upgrade 92km existing earthen/gravel road between Muyembe and Nakapiripirit towns to asphaltic paved road standard and improve 25km secondary road links.

title: Upgrading of Rwenkunye–Apac–Lira–Acholibur Road Project
short_description: UPGRADING OF RWENKUNYE–APAC–LIRA–ACHOLIBUR ROAD PROJECT
long_description: The objective of the project is to facilitate the socio­ economic development of Uganda by upgrading the road link between Rwenkunye- Apac- Lira - Acholibur by improving road surface, road safety and increasing the overall efficiency of road transport system.The development of this road will enhance Uganda's competitiveness as a regional hub linking the neighboring countries. The completed project road will facilitate long haul fast movement of freight as well as support economic development of strategic sectors like agriculture, mining, tourism and oil and gas.

title: Center-West Region Roads project
short_description: CENTER-WEST REGION ROADS PROJECT
long_description: The objective of the project is to stimulate economic growth of the country and contribute indirectly to the objectives of the government's Poverty Reduction Strategy Program. The project would contribute to the economic and social development of the center-west region and increase the competitiveness of the Ivoirian economy in the West African sub-region. On the international level, the project would contribute to promoting regional integration between Cote d'Ivoire, Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone.

title: Construction of the Darakokaha-Kanawolo-Tafiré Highway Project (96.3 km)
short_description: CONSTRUCTION OF THE DARAKOKAHA-KANAWOLO-TAFIRÉ HIGHWAY PROJECT (96.3 KM)
long_description: The development goals will contribute to economic growth by building sustainable infrastructure that supports international, regional and national connectivity, in line with the PND 2021-2025 to achieve economic growth over 7% per year for the next five years.The specific project objectives are to improve the level of service and accommodate traffic growth along the A3 national and regional corridor linking the autonomous Port of Abidjan, a maritime entrance to Côte d'Ivoire, to the neighboring countries to the north without maritime facades, Burkina Faso and Mali.  The key results expected from the traffic growth and improved level of service are:a. Travel time between Darakokaha and Tafiré reduced for light vehicles: from 73 minutes in 2024 to 50 minutes in 2030, Travel time between Darakokaha and Tafiré reduced for heavy goods vehicles: from 82 minutes in 2024 to 64 minutes in 2030,b. VOC costs between Darakokaha and Tafiré for light vehicles: from 0.27 Euro/veh-km in 2024 to 0.26 Euro/veh-km in 2030. Reduction in VOC between Darakokaha and Tafiré for heavy goods vehicles: by 1.08 Euro/veh-km in 2024 for heavy goods vehicles at 0.88 Euro/veh-km in 2030.c. Average Annual Daily Traffic (AADT) between Darakokaha and Kanawolo: from 2,900 veh/d in 2024 to 4,200 veh/d in 2030, TMAA between Kanawolo and Tafir

title: Widening of Bertil–Harding Highway Phase III, Gambia
short_description: WIDENING OF BERTIL–HARDING HIGHWAY PHASE III, GAMBIA
long_description: The primary objective of this project is to improve the overall functionality of the Bertil Harding Highway through the implementation of an additional overpass as well as the construction of service roads equipped with essential drainage and road furniture aiming to promote mobility and safety. The successful implementation of the service roads will boost the country's economy and will promote easy access to residential and commercial amenities.

donor: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
donor_original: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Montenegrin Railways Passenger Trains
short_description: MONTENEGRIN RAILWAYS PASSENGER TRAINS
long_description: The provision of a senior loan of up to € 30 million to  Zeljezniiki Prevoz Crne Gore a.d. (ZPCG), the passenger train operator in Montenegro. The loan will be fully guaranteed by the State of Montenegro (the Guarantor) and used to finance the purchase 4-car Electro-motor unit (EMU) sets to be used for operations across the railway network of Montenegro. The new rolling stock will replace the old, energy inefficient loco-hauled train sets currently in operation.

donor: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
donor_original: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: ASCO Caspian Shipping
short_description: ASCO CASPIAN SHIPPING
long_description: A senior secured loan (the 'Loan') to CJSC Azerbaijan Caspian Shipping Company (the 'Company' or 'ASCO') to support the acquisition of two handy-size dry cargo vessels as part of its international expansion strategy.
